channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Cavaliers rely on scoring balance to beat Bucks 112-100 for 14th consecutive victory
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Max Strus had 17 points to lead a balanced scoring attack as the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Milwaukee Bucks 112-100 on Sunday night for their 14th consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drawing huge crowds, Bernie Sanders steps into leadership of the anti-Trump resistance
item.description: WARREN, Mich. (AP) — Bernie Sanders is standing alone on the back of a pickup truck shouting into a bullhorn. He's facing several hundred ecstatic voters huddled outside a suburban Detroit high school — the group that did not fit inside the high school's gym or two overflow rooms.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Court-martial hearing set to begin for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents, is scheduled to begin his court-martial on additional charges Monday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How to Stream Roberta Flack's 'Celebration of Life' memorial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Roberta Flack, the Grammy-winning singer and pianist whose songs “The First Time Ever I Saw Your Face" and “Killing Me Softly with His Song" made her a global star, will be eulogized at a memorial service Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Immigration agents arrest Palestinian activist who helped lead Columbia University protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal immigration authorities arrested a Palestinian activist Saturday who played a prominent role in Columbia University’s protests against Israel, a significant escalation in the Trump administration’s pledge to detain and deport student activists.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Scholars stranded in America and abroad amid funding freeze of State Department programs
item.description: Fulbright scholar Aubrey Lay was supposed to get paid for three months of work by the U.S. government through his teaching assistantship at a school for Ukrainian refugees in Estonia. Instead, he only got about one week's pay and no word on when he might see the rest of his grant.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump pushes to shut down the Dept. of Education: What impact it has on Florida
item.description: Florida relies on billions in Federal education funding, from special education to student loan programs.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope thanks volunteers for 'miracle of tenderness' as another Holy Year event passes without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis thanked volunteers Sunday for the “miracle of tenderness” they offer the sick, as he continued his treatment for double pneumonia and doctors reported some positive news.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plane crashes in retirement community's parking lot in Pennsylvania: officials
item.description: A plane carrying five people crashed in the parking lot of the Brethren Village retirement community near Lititz, Pennsylvania, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pitt student vanishes on spring break trip to Dominican Republic while walking on beach
item.description: A University of Pittsburgh student has vanished while on a spring break trip to Dominican Republic, and searches are currently underway.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firefighters knock down wind-driven brush fire on Long Island but risk remains with high winds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Firefighters in New York knocked down a dwindling brush fire in a wooded stretch of Long Island on Sunday as officials warned that high wind gusts could leave the region vulnerable to additional blazes. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Robert Pattison sci-fi ‘Mickey 17’ opens in first place, but profitability is a long way off
item.description: “Parasite” filmmaker Bong Joon Ho’s original science fiction film “Mickey 17” opened in first place on the North American box office charts.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Courts grapple with 'King' Trump
item.description: The White House sees few, if any, limits on President Trump's executive powers in his second term, but the federal court system is much less sure.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: FBI’s DNA tactics didn’t violate law, but they raise another public safety concern
item.description: IGG evidence in the Bryan Kohberger investigation was obtained controversially but legally. Expert CeCe Moore explains why there are lingering concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 hurt when driver plows vehicle into Southern California car dealership
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Eight people were injured Saturday when a driver plowed a vehicle into a CarMax location in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: International Women's Day protests demand equal rights and an end to discrimination, sexual violence
item.description: Women took to the streets of cities across Europe, Africa, South America and elsewhere to mark International Women’s Day with demands for ending inequality and gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey police officer killed, second officer wounded in shooting by 14-year-old, authorities say
item.description: A 14-year-old shot and killed a police officer and wounded another Friday evening in New Jersey, authorities said, in a chaotic scene that witnesses described as seeing officers running down a busy street before they heard a volley of a dozen or more gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Total lunar eclipse, blood moon will be visible in March
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail to return to theaters
item.description: The movie, which first hit theaters in 1975, will return to the big screen for a limited time in celebration of its 50th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US repatriating 135 migrants daily to Mexico on flights
item.description: As of March 3, Mexico's government says it has received 19,663 deported migrants since January 20 when President Trump took office, including 4,052 who were not Mexican nationals.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that killed seven people when it plummeted into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January and likely had not been functioning for several years, the National Transportation Safety Board said in a preliminary rep
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana and Arkansas look to nitrogen executions
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Hours after the nation’s first execution by nitrogen gas in January last year, Alabama's attorney general urged other states to also develop it as a method for carrying out death sentences. Now, some states are following suit.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, KIM CHANDLER and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4
